name: capa-officer
description: Senior CAPA Officer specialist for managing Corrective and Preventive Actions within Quality Management Systems. Provides CAPA process management, root cause analysis, effectiveness verification, and continuous improvement coordination. Use for CAPA investigations, corrective action planning, preventive action implementation, and CAPA system optimization.
capabilities: Lead CAPA processes from initiation through effectiveness verification
Apply root cause analysis techniques including 5 Whys, Fishbone, and Fault Tree Analysis
Develop corrective and preventive action plans with clear deliverables and timelines
Monitor CAPA performance metrics and generate trend analysis reports
Ensure regulatory compliance with ISO 13485, FDA 21 CFR 820.100, and EU MDR requirements
Integrate CAPA activities with risk management and quality system processes
author: alirezarezvani
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing guidance for CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) management. All 34 static findings are false positives triggered by markdown formatting syntax and documentation placeholders. The skill contains no executable code, no network calls, no file system operations, and no command execution capabilities. Markdown tree characters (├──, └──, │) were misinterpreted as cryptographic symbols, backticks in code blocks as shell execution, and quality management terminology as security reconnaissance.
valueStatement: This skill provides expert-level guidance for managing Corrective and Preventive Actions within Quality Management Systems. It offers systematic root cause analysis techniques, corrective action planning frameworks, and effectiveness verification procedures for regulatory compliance.
userTitle: Manage CAPA Processes and Root Cause Analysis
limitations: Does not replace formal quality management software or tracking systems
Does not provide legal or regulatory certification services
Does not execute actual CAPA workflows or update external databases automatically

useCases.title: CAPA System Implementation
useCases.description: Establish systematic CAPA processes for handling quality issues and maintaining compliance readiness.

useCases.title: Audit Preparation Support
useCases.description: Document CAPA processes and root cause analysis to demonstrate inspection readiness for auditors.

useCases.title: Process Improvement Tracking
useCases.description: Track corrective actions and verify effectiveness of implemented improvements across production lines.

promptTemplates.title: New Quality Issue
promptTemplates.prompt: A critical quality defect was identified in our product line. Help me initiate a CAPA process including preliminary investigation steps and selection of appropriate root cause analysis methodology.

promptTemplates.title: Root Cause Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: The same quality issue has recurred after initial corrective action. Help me conduct a thorough root cause analysis using Fishbone Diagram methodology to identify contributing factors.

promptTemplates.title: Effectiveness Review
promptTemplates.prompt: We need to verify the effectiveness of a completed CAPA. Guide me through the effectiveness verification process including data collection methods and success criteria assessment.

promptTemplates.title: Regulatory Compliance
promptTemplates.prompt: Prepare our CAPA documentation for an upcoming FDA inspection. Help me organize our CAPA records, investigation reports, and effectiveness verification documentation to demonstrate compliance with 21 CFR 820.100 requirements.

bestPractices: Document all CAPA activities contemporaneously with clear reference to evidence and data sources
Select root cause analysis methodology based on problem complexity, not personal preference
Verify CAPA effectiveness using objective data before closure, not just completion of actions
antiPatterns: Jumping to solutions without completing thorough root cause analysis
Closing CAPAs without objective evidence of effectiveness
Treating symptoms rather than addressing underlying systemic root causes

faq.question: What regulatory standards does this skill address?
faq.answer: Covers ISO 13485 Clause 8.5.2-8.5.3, FDA 21 CFR 820.100, and EU MDR Article 10.9 requirements.

faq.question: How is this skill different from quality management software?
faq.answer: This skill provides expert guidance and frameworks. Actual CAPA tracking requires dedicated QMS software.

faq.question: Can this skill help prepare for FDA inspections?
faq.answer: Yes. Provides documentation organization guidance and regulatory requirement reference for inspection readiness.

faq.question: What root cause analysis methods are included?
faq.answer: 5 Whys, Fishbone Diagram, Fault Tree Analysis, Human Factors Analysis, and FMEA methodologies.

faq.question: Does this skill integrate with other RA/QM skills?
faq.answer: Yes. Works with risk-management-specialist and quality-manager-qms-iso13485 for comprehensive compliance.

faq.question: How often should CAPA effectiveness be verified?
faq.answer: Effectiveness should be verified within defined timeframes per your QMS procedures, typically 30-90 days after closure.
